game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Meridian 157: Kapitel 2:** Players navigate a series of pre-rendered, static environments by tapping on directional arrows to move between scenes, and tapping on objects or areas to interact with them, collecting items and solving puzzles. The core loop involves exploring, identifying environmental puzzles, and using an inventory system where collected items can be combined by dragging one onto another to create new tools or solutions. Puzzles range from manipulating objects and deciphering codes to applying inventory items in specific contexts, with a logical hint system available for assistance. Progression is linear, driven by successfully solving each puzzle to unlock new areas and advance the overarching narrative of escaping the island. The game concludes upon completing the final puzzle and achieving the escape objective, with no explicit failure state beyond being stuck on a puzzle.
game_info.description: Escape from Meridian Island by exploring mysterious environments, solving intricate puzzles, and uncovering secrets. Navigate through interconnected scenes, collect and combine items in your inventory, decipher cryptic codes, and manipulate objects to unlock new areas. Each puzzle you solve brings you closer to freedom. Your goal: reach the final escape location and complete your journey off the island.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between scenes (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT when arrows are visible)
Z Key: Interact with highlighted objects or use selected inventory items
Space: Select/deselect items in inventory
Shift: Combine selected inventory items
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (return to start screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic navigation and interaction mechanics. Validates that the player can move between scenes using arrow keys and interact with objects using the Z key. Ensures the game responds correctly to directional inputs and object interactions.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Explores the initial scenes by moving in all available directions. Interacts with visible objects to test pickup mechanics. Validates that inventory updates correctly when items are collected.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully navigates through multiple scenes, collects at least one item, and confirms that the game state updates appropriately. The test succeeds when basic movement and interaction are verified without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the complete win path by solving all puzzles in optimal sequence. Validates that all puzzle mechanics work correctly and that the win condition is achievable. Ensures the game progresses logically from start to escape.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Follows the optimal solution path: collect key items, combine them when needed, use items on specific objects, solve code puzzles, and navigate to the escape location. Uses knowledge of puzzle solutions to complete the game efficiently.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Reaches GAME_OVER_WIN state by successfully solving all puzzles and reaching the final escape scene. The test succeeds when the game transitions to the win screen with the escape complete message.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests inventory management including item selection, deselection, and combination mechanics. Validates that items can be properly selected with Space and combined with Shift, creating new composite items.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Collects multiple items from different scenes, selects them individually using Space, attempts valid combinations using Shift, and verifies that combined items replace their components in the inventory.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Successfully demonstrates inventory operations: selecting items highlights them, combining compatible items creates new items, and the inventory state remains consistent throughout. Test succeeds when at least one item combination is completed.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests puzzle-solving mechanics including code input and object manipulation. Validates that puzzles accept correct solutions and reject incorrect ones, with appropriate feedback and state changes.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Attempts to solve environmental puzzles by entering codes, using items on specific objects, and manipulating interactive elements. Tests both correct and incorrect solutions to verify proper validation.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Puzzles respond correctly to solutions: correct inputs unlock new areas or provide items, incorrect inputs give feedback without breaking game state. Test succeeds when at least two different puzzles are solved.
